mission.title: Install and Configure Cozystack on Kubernetes
mission.description: Cozystack is a free PaaS platform that simplifies the management of virtual machines, Kubernetes clusters, and databases. Installing Cozystack allows you to build and manage your own cloud infrastructure seamlessly.

steps.title: Clone the Cozystack repository
steps.description:
Start by cloning the Cozystack repository to your local machine using the following command:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/cozystack/cozystack.git
cd cozystack
```

steps.title: Apply the Kubernetes manifests
steps.description:
Deploy Cozystack to your Kubernetes cluster by applying the provided manifests:
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/namespace.yaml
kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml --namespace cozystack
kubectl apply -f k8s/service.yaml --namespace cozystack
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Cozystack pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cozystack
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the Cozystack service
steps.description:
To access the Cozystack service locally, run:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/cozystack-service 8080:80 --namespace cozystack
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Cozystack deployment by editing the deployment manifest:
```bash
kubectl edit deployment cozystack --namespace cozystack
# Add resource limits under spec.containers.resources
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Cozystack deployment
uninstall.description:
Delete the Cozystack service and deployment:
```bash
kubectl delete service cozystack-service --namespace cozystack
kubectl delete deployment cozystack --namespace cozystack
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Cozystack namespace
uninstall.description:
Clean up by deleting the Cozystack namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cozystack
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of your Cozystack deployment:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace cozystack -o yaml > cozystack-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the repository
upgrade.description:
Pull the latest changes from the Cozystack repository:
```bash
git pull origin main
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the deployment
upgrade.description:
Reapply the updated manifests to upgrade Cozystack:
```bash
kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml --namespace cozystack
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure the upgrade was successful:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cozystack
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Cozystack pods are not starting, check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace cozystack
``` 
Ensure that the container images are accessible and that there are no resource constraints.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Cozystack service, verify the service configuration:
```bash
kubectl describe svc cozystack-service --namespace cozystack
``` 
Make sure the service type is set correctly (e.g., ClusterIP, NodePort).

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource allocation errors, check the resource limits set in the deployment:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment cozystack --namespace cozystack
``` 
Adjust the resource limits as necessary.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Cozystack will have all pods running in the 'cozystack' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via port-forwarding. You can also verify resource limits and configurations as needed.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f k8s/namespace.yaml
kubectl apply -f k8s/deployment.yaml --namespace cozystack
kubectl apply -f k8s/service.yaml --namespace cozystack

kubectl edit deployment cozystack --namespace cozystack
# Add resource limits under spec.containers.resources

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and kubectl configured to interact with it.
